title: Racosperma evenulosum
taxonomy: Racosperma evenulosum, also known as Acacia evenulosum, is a species of shrub in the family Fabaceae. This classification places it within the order Fabales and the subfamily Mimosoideae. The genus Racosperma is often considered a synonym for Acacia, although some sources may still recognize it as a distinct genus. The classification may also group it within the infraclass Rosidae. Further research may be needed to confirm the most current and widely accepted classification of Racosperma evenulosum.
uses: Racosperma evenulosum, commonly known as the Sydney golden wattle, has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
